zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Sandford於1260年出生在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德阿斯哈姆時，他的父親Richard de Sandford42歲，母親Eleanor Cadigan41歲。他於1285年與Agnes Gundreda de Westmoreland在英國維斯特莫藍德阿斯哈姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他於1316年被列為represented the county almost continuously in 16 parliaments。他去世於1356年享年96歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sandford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England with names derived from Old English sand ‘sand’ + ford ‘ford, river crossing’, such as Sandford in Warcop (Westmorland), Sandford in Sonning and Dry Sandford in Abingdon (both Berkshire), Sandford on Thames and Sandford Saint Martin (both Oxfordshire), Great and Little Sampford (Essex), Sandford Orcas (Dorset), Sandford, Sampford Courtney, Sampford Peverell, and Sampford Spiney (all Devon), Sampford Arundel and Sampford Brett (both Somerset), and Sandford (Shropshire). See also Sandiford .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Sandford was born in 1260, in Askham, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de Sandford, was 42 and his mother, Eleanor Cadigan, was 41. He married Agnes Gundreda de Westmoreland in 1285, in Askham, Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. In 1316, his occupation is listed as represented the county almost continuously in 16 parliaments. He died in 1356, at the age of 96.

Il a épousé Agnes Gundreda de Westmoreland en 1285, à Askham, Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. En 1316, sa profession de son indiquée est represented the county almost continuously in 16 parliaments. Il est décédé en 1356, à l’âge de 96 ans.
